research_topics: Claude Fundamental BUY bias root cause
Signal correlation pruning and ensemble methods

findings.topic: Claude Fundamental BUY Bias (ROOT CAUSE FOUND)
findings.finding: Sonnet tier has 83% BUY rate, Opus tier has 78% BUY rate over last 500 entries (Apr 21-24). Haiku correctly abstains (96% HOLD). The cascade logic prefers highest-tier non-HOLD vote, so Sonnet/Opus's structural optimism overrides Haiku's prudent abstention. Result: signal outputs BUY ~33% with ZERO SELL for crypto/metals. In ranging market, this is consistently wrong (37.9% recent 1d accuracy, down from 59.4%).
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL — claude_fundamental is our most expensive signal (CLI subprocess) and has collapsed from top-5 to bottom-5 accuracy

findings.finding: Recent paper (MDPI Electronics 15/6/1334) shows regime-aware LightGBM with rolling Hidden Markov Model for regime detection outperforms non-regime-conditioned models on NASDAQ over 10 years. Key insight: aggregate statistics mask regime-dependent signal performance. Our accuracy_cache confirms this — some signals are 60%+ in trending but 40% in ranging.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH — all our tickers are currently 'ranging' and our worst-performing signals (structure 32.6%, smart_money 32.7%) are trend-following signals that fail in range-bound markets

findings.finding: FactSet practical guide shows Max Information Ratio weighting outperforms equal weighting over 13 years. Formula: weight_i = IC_i / sigma_i (information coefficient divided by tracking error). LLM-enhanced alpha discovery achieves 75% improvement in IC over baseline RL (avg IC=0.0515). Key: use rolling IC, not static.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH — directly applicable. Our memory/quant_research_priorities.md already has IC-based weighting as #1 priority. Implementation plan exists.

findings.finding: Best practice: transform signals to mean-stationary, standardize, then compress with PCA to reduce redundancy. Coefficient of variation across walk-forward windows detects overfitting. Shannon entropy filtering improves signal quality by removing noise. Cross-correlation-based pruning identifies redundant signal pairs.
findings.relevance_to_us: MEDIUM — we have 51 signal modules but many are likely correlated (e.g., trend + heikin_ashi, momentum + momentum_factors). PCA-based pruning could reduce noise. But our disabled signals already output HOLD, so the main issue is HOLD dilution rather than correlated active signals.

findings.finding: TauricResearch's TradingAgents v0.2.0 (Feb 2026) uses bull/bear researcher debate, risk management team, and trader synthesis. Supports multi-provider LLMs. Key architecture: structured debates between bullish/bearish agents surface blind spots. Reported improvements in Sharpe ratio and max drawdown vs single-agent.
findings.relevance_to_us: LOW-MEDIUM — our Layer 2 is single-agent. Adding bull/bear debate would be a significant architecture change. Could be tested in a separate branch. Main value: the debate mechanism could fix the claude_fundamental BUY bias by adding a bear counterargument.

findings.topic: Silver Price Prediction: ARIMA vs ML
findings.finding: Recent IJSR study (2000-2025 daily data) found ARIMA outperforms Random Forest and XGBoost for silver short-term forecasting. ML models lose accuracy during high-volatility periods. Key features: Fed policy, USD strength, industrial demand (solar/EV), inflation expectations, gold-silver ratio.
findings.relevance_to_us: MEDIUM — our Chronos forecast for XAG has regressed to ~50% accuracy. ARIMA might be a simpler, more robust alternative for short-term silver direction. But our main edge on XAG is at 3h (54.5%) not 1d (51.2%), so the short-term forecasting approach aligns.

findings.finding: Novel double out-of-sample WFO approach (arXiv 2602.10785) adds a second validation layer. Key insight: compute coefficient of variation for optimized parameters across walk-forward windows — high variation = overfitting. Stability penalty encourages consistent parameters.
findings.relevance_to_us: MEDIUM — applicable to our signal weight optimization. Currently we use static 70/30 recency weighting. Walk-forward would adaptively reweight signals based on recent performance while detecting when parameter drift indicates regime change.

findings.finding: SAGE journal study shows sentiment indicators improve algo trading performance only when combined with technical signals, not standalone. Our sentiment signal at 33.8% recent 3h and 45.9% 1d confirms this — it's actively anti-correlated with correct direction. CryptoBERT and keyword-based sentiment may be capturing noise rather than signal.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH — sentiment is one of our highest-volume signals (39K+ samples) and consistently bad. Should be force-HOLD immediately rather than allowed to vote.

recommended_improvements.title: Fix claude_fundamental BUY bias via regime-aware cascade
recommended_improvements.description: In ranging regime, prefer Haiku (which correctly abstains 96% of the time) over Sonnet/Opus (which have 78-83% BUY bias). Add a tier_accuracy tracker that measures per-tier hit rate and disables tiers below 45%. Alternatively, require Sonnet/Opus to have majority BUY across sub_signals (not just any non-HOLD) before overriding Haiku.

recommended_improvements.title: Force-HOLD sentiment signal
recommended_improvements.description: Add 'sentiment' to DISABLED_SIGNALS in signal_engine.py. At 33.8% recent 3h and 45.9% 1d with 39K+ samples, it's proven anti-edge. The accuracy gate at 47% should already catch it, but verify it's actually being gated.

recommended_improvements.title: Force-HOLD structure signal
recommended_improvements.description: Structure signal collapsed to 32.6% recent 1d. Add to DISABLED_SIGNALS or verify accuracy gate is catching it.

recommended_improvements.title: Implement rolling IC-based signal weighting
recommended_improvements.description: Replace equal-weight voting with IC-weighted voting. Use 7-day rolling Information Coefficient per signal per ticker. Signals with negative IC get zero weight (not inverted). This is the #1 quant research priority from memory.

recommended_improvements.title: Add regime-conditioned accuracy tracking
recommended_improvements.description: Split accuracy_cache by regime (trending/ranging/high-vol). This enables regime-dependent signal selection — only activate signals that perform well in the current regime.

recommended_improvements.title: Reduce HOLD dilution from disabled signals
recommended_improvements.description: 18 disabled signals output HOLD and inflate total_voters. They should be excluded from voter count entirely, not counted as HOLD voters. This would increase consensus percentages and reduce noise.
